_comment: HOW THIS WORKS: 'latest' is the version AVAILABLE to users. The running build is CURRENT_VERSION inside wealthflow-update-system.js. To announce an update: publish new app files AND set 'latest' here to the new number. Users on an older version see the 'Update available' banner. 'type' = full | minor | security. List a version in 'mandatory' to force it.

notes.7.45.0: Sharper transaction intelligence and a real fix for the diagnostics build tag. The AI router that decides each payment's destination, type and category now knows far more of Sri Lanka — 40+ additional real merchants and services across Groceries, Dining, Transport, Utilities, Health, Shopping, Entertainment, Education and Insurance (Keells Super, Arpico Supercentre, Softlogic GLOMARK, Nawaloka, Asiri, Durdans, Scope Cinema, Majestic Cineplex, Kangaroo Cabs, PEO TV, Lanka Bell, Vijitha Yapa, University of Moratuwa, LOLC/Takaful insurance and many more), so more charges land in the right tab and category automatically instead of falling to a generic 'Other'. Every one of these was verified against 66 classification tests, with the full original set re-checked so nothing regressed. All AI engines already run fully in parallel and vote on the best answer — that is confirmed working. Also fixed: the diagnostics report was showing build 'unknown' because it read the version from a page element that loads far later than the code that needed it; the app now carries an authoritative build constant, so every recorded error is correctly stamped and the log clears itself cleanly on each update. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.44.0: Smarter diagnostics that never show stale errors again, the Daily Notification Limit fixed, and the Google Drive picker now shows only files YOU uploaded, newest first. Diagnostics upgrade: every recorded error is now stamped with the exact app build, and the log wipes itself automatically whenever you update — so old errors from a previous version can never look like new ones (that is what made the last two reports look like the crash was still happening when it was already fixed). The copied report now includes a summary — total errors, how many are from THIS build vs stale, and the distinct messages — plus whether you are running as an installed app, so problems are far easier to pinpoint. There is also a new 'Clear log' button next to 'Copy diagnostics'. Daily Notification Limit fixed: the daily counter never reset at midnight — it kept climbing day after day until it silently blocked your smart notifications. It now resets correctly each day and honours your chosen limit exactly. Google Drive picker: it now lists only the files YOU uploaded or own (not files merely shared with you), newest upload first, as a plain list with folders excluded — so your latest statement is right at the top. Note: if the app logs you out after signing in through the browser, that is because an installed app has its own private storage separate from Safari — sign in once inside the app itself and it stays signed in. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.43.0: The real fix for the Expenses tab, the iPhone crash solved from your Diagnostics, statement Undo that now removes EVERYTHING, and a smarter Google Drive picker. Expenses tab — real root cause found and fixed: an internal month list was used but never actually created, so the tab could stop drawing and your expenses would not appear, whether you added them by hand OR from a statement. That list is now built correctly, so every expense shows immediately, grouped by month, with the month filter working again. The crash is solved: the Diagnostics you sent pinpointed it exactly — it was never a scroll problem. On iPhone the browser tab has no notification system, and one line assumed it did, throwing the same error over and over on the dashboard. That entire class of error is now impossible, on every page. Undo a statement now removes EVERYTHING it added: undoing a statement import used to leave behind cheques, card payments and loan instalments. Undo now cleanly removes every record the import created — expenses, received income, card charges, card payments, cheques AND loan instalments — and restores anything it had replaced. Google Drive shows your newest file first: the picker no longer puts folders on top. It shows one list of your files from every folder, most-recently-changed first, so your latest statement is right at the top. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.42.0: Google Drive import fixed, the Expenses tab made bullet-proof, a false 'Offline' on refresh fixed, and a new one-tap way to file a whole statement. Google Drive: the 'From Drive' picker was showing 'The API developer key is invalid' — it now opens reliably using your Google sign-in alone, with no key to misconfigure and nothing sensitive exposed to the browser. If Drive still doesn't open for you, the only remaining switch is on Google's side: make sure BOTH the Google Drive API and the Google Picker API are enabled on your Google Cloud project. Refresh no longer flips Online to Offline: tapping the Sync button at the top used to show 'Offline' whenever a page had trouble drawing, even though your connection was perfectly fine and live sync was still running. A drawing hiccup can now never change your connection status — the badge reflects your real connection only. Expenses tab made bullet-proof: a single odd record from an old import could stop the whole Expenses tab from drawing (and, through that refresh bug, look like you'd gone offline). Every expense is now safety-checked as the tab loads, so bad data can't knock it out again. File a whole statement in one tap: the statement review screen now has a 'Send every ticked row to' control — if charges landed in the wrong place, pick Expenses (or any tab) and apply it to all ticked rows at once, then fine-tune any single row if you like. Diagnostics: Settings now has a 'Copy diagnostics' button — if anything ever crashes or a page misbehaves, tap it right after and send me the result so the exact cause can be fixed fast. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.41.0: Cleaner undo, a smoother hold animation, undo that finally sticks, and Google Drive import in every upload area. Undo that sticks: when you press-and-hold to send a confirmed investment month back to Pending, it now stays undone after you refresh or reopen the app — previously it could quietly come back because the change never fully reached the cloud. Hold-to-undo, refined: the 'hold to undo' hint is now one clean, consistent size from the moment a card loads (no more large-then-small jump), it uses a crisp undo icon instead of a text symbol, and the 3-second press sweeps a polished red fill with a glowing edge and a gentle press-in — on the whole green 'Received & Confirmed' button and on any confirmed row inside 'Past months'. A quick tap still does nothing and scrolling still cancels the hold, so it never fires by accident. From Drive: you can now import a statement or receipt straight from your Google Drive. Look for the new 'From Drive' button beside Upload File in Settings > Statement Upload, Expenses, Credit Card one-time charges, and Subscriptions — pick a PDF, an HTML e-statement or a photo and it runs through the exact same smart scan as a normal upload (perfect for statements your bank emailed you and you saved to Drive). Small polish: the camera 'Photo' buttons now use a clean icon too. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.40.0: Undo a confirmed investment month, a fixed Restore history list, and a Drive connection that stays on. Investments: confirmed a month by mistake? Press and hold the green 'Received & Confirmed' button on an investment card for 3 seconds — a fill sweeps across it and that month goes back to Pending. It also works inside 'Past months': press and hold any confirmed month row (e.g. 'May 2026') for 3 seconds to undo just that month (each confirmed row now shows a 'hold to undo' hint). A quick tap does nothing and scrolling cancels the hold, so it never fires by accident. Restore Data Protocol: fixed the empty backup-history list — after backing up to Google Drive, Settings > Restore Data Protocol now reliably shows every backup this account made, newest first, with date/time and size. The old list only looked inside one Drive folder, so backups elsewhere were invisible; it now finds them wherever they are, and the backup you just made shows immediately. Google Drive now stays connected on stand-by for the whole session and refreshes silently in the background, so Restore and Instant Cloud Backup keep working without asking you to sign in again — until you sign out of your Google account. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.39.0: Expenses, notifications and settings polish. Expenses: when you tap a category to view its history, the transactions now appear as a clearly nested thread (indented under the category with a connecting rail and a 'Transactions in …' label) so you can tell the category header from the individual charges — like a comment and its replies. The 'mark paid' tick now appears only on manually-added expenses, and only for the current and upcoming months; bank-statement expenses no longer show a tick (they're records of money already spent — shown as 'Recorded' in the table view), and when a new month begins last month's manual expenses are auto-marked paid. Notifications: each item is now colour-coded by reason — overdue is red, due-today/imminent is orange, due-soon is gold, system/info is blue. Settings: turning an option on/off no longer makes the page jump or 'dance' — toggles flip instantly in place and keep your scroll position. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.38.0: Notifications, perfected. Fixed: seen notifications now stay seen — the unseen badge no longer resets when you reopen the app, and it persists across reloads. Fixed: on phones in portrait the panel was running off the left edge (title clipped to 'ifications', left icons cut) — it's now anchored to the screen with safe margins and notch-safe insets, so it always fits. Improved: a summary line shows how many items need attention, notifications are grouped into 'Needs attention', 'Coming up' and 'Good to know' (each with a count), every item shows a due chip ('4d over', 'Today', 'in 3d', 'This mo'), and the styling is more refined. New: optional device reminders — turn on Settings → Notifications → Device push notifications and WealthFlow will remind you in your phone/computer notification centre when a payment or cheque needs attention (deduplicated so it never nags twice; tapping opens the right page). The bell still only reads your data, never changes it. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.37.0: New Notifications centre in the top bar. A bell with a red badge shows how many urgent + warning items you haven't seen. It surfaces what needs attention from your real data: overdue/due-today cheques, unpaid card payments near or past deadline, loan instalments due this month or overdue, and bills/subscriptions due soon or overdue (active card instalments show as info). Most urgent first, newest at the top. Tap a notification to jump straight to the right page; opening the bell clears the unseen count; opening or tapping an item marks it seen. Settings → Notifications adds a master on/off switch plus per-category toggles (urgent, due-soon, cheques, card payments, loans, bills, card instalments). The bell only reads your data — it never changes it. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.36.0: Year Income now counts the REAL money you received, not the Investments tab. It reads your Income page (bank-statement imports + manual entries) and excludes the Investments page. Why: a recurring investment yield (e.g. 100,000/month) was tracked on Investments AND, when that same money hit your bank and the statement was imported, captured again as received income — so it was being counted twice. Year Income now reads only the received side, so the same money can never double-count. The fix flows through everything that shows income: the dashboard Year Income card, Net Savings, Savings Rate, the monthly income chart, the Monthly Plan, the CSV + PDF reports, and the 3D Cash Flow view. Your Investments page is 100% intact — it just no longer inflates Year Income. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.35.0: Three fixes you asked for. (1) Settings → Software Update → 'View' / 'What's new' now opens the release-notes sheet correctly — it was silently doing nothing whenever the notes were stored as plain text. (2) Removed the 'Reduce my Balance Tracker' checkbox from '+ Add Saving' — it had no effect (your saving is recorded against the target; your balance is moved separately from 'Log Balance Flow → Saving for Target'), so it was only adding confusion. (3) On small phone screens the CC One-Time sort controls (↓ Newest / ↑ Oldest, default ↓ Newest) now stay compact so the '+ Add Payment' button no longer drops onto a second line. Reminder: passcode-free entry for the installed app/APK already lives in Settings → Device Access & Encryption → 'Passcode-free entry (installed app only)' — it's off by default for safety and only ever applies to the installed app (never a browser tab); turn it ON once and the installed app opens straight to your dashboard. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.34.0: Bank-statement cheques now file straight into your Cheque tab — accurately, for every bank and every wording. A deposited / inward / realised cheque is booked as RECEIVED (money in); a cheque payment / outward / honoured cheque is booked as ISSUED (money out); and the cheque number is read automatically with leading zeros preserved (e.g. 'Transfer Cheque Deposit Cheque No: 070283' files as Received, #070283). Detection now covers deposit / inward / outward / clearing / realised / lodgement / collection / local / upcountry / outstation / electronic cheque, plus shorthand like 'chq dep', 'cq no', 'chque' and '#070283'. A cheque can no longer be swallowed by the internal-transfer skip — rows like 'Internal Transfer – Cheque Deposit' are recognised as cheques first, so they always reach the Cheque tab. Cheque-book / leaf / return / dishonour fees are still treated as bank charges, never as a cheque movement, and received-vs-issued is decided by the wording first, then the credit/debit column. Newly imported statement cheques arrive marked 'cleared' and de-duplicate by cheque number, so re-importing never creates duplicates. This is a detection upgrade in the import engine — your existing cheques are untouched. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.33.0: Bank-statement loan repayments now land on the EXACTLY right loan, even when you keep several. If you have two housing loans plus a vehicle lease, WealthFlow tells them apart by loan TYPE first (a vehicle/lease payment can NEVER be filed against a housing loan), then by the monthly instalment amount (which separates two same-type loans), then decisively by the loan/account number or name when the statement prints it. Zero silent mistakes: when a statement genuinely is not specific enough, the row is shown for your one-tap confirmation in the import review instead of being filed against the wrong loan. The instant classifier now also recognises far more health spending (HEALTH, doctor, medicine, dispensary, e-Channelling, doc990, dental, optical and more), so health charges stop falling into Other even when OCR truncates the text, and the 18-engine AI vote (all engines in parallel) got a richer Sri Lankan merchant brief and reads abbreviated statement text more intelligently. The AI still only ever upgrades a generic guess. Backend/engine update; no app screens changed.
notes.7.32.0: WealthFlow's classification brain now consults up to 18 AI engines at once — xAI Grok, Anthropic Claude, DeepInfra, Hyperbolic, Novita, OpenAI and Cohere join the existing line-up (Gemini, Groq, DeepSeek, Mistral, Together, Fireworks, OpenRouter, Cerebras, SambaNova, NVIDIA, GitHub Models). Every configured engine runs IN PARALLEL and decides each ambiguous charge by majority vote, so 'Goes to' and 'Type' are correct even more often — and the AI still only ever upgrades a generic guess, so locked fuel/cash-advance/fee verdicts are never overridden. The built-in knowledge base is now in lock-step with the import classifier (many more Sri Lankan merchants, fees, taxes, levies and fuel stations recognised instantly and offline), app modules always refresh on a new release, and the server now gives the multi-engine vote its full time budget so even slower engines still weigh in. This is a backend/engine update — no app screens changed.
notes.7.31.0: Open the installed app (home-screen / APK) straight to your dashboard — no passcode or biometric — via a new Settings → Device Access toggle. For your security this only ever applies in the installed app, never in a browser tab, and it is off by default. While it is on, the installed app also will not auto-lock on inactivity (a browser tab still locks normally). Your PIN and biometrics are untouched and still protect a browser tab and every other device.
notes.7.30.0: Loans now reconcile themselves, including telling apart multiple loans (e.g. two housing loans + a vehicle lease) by amount, bank, name and — decisively — the loan/account number. Cheques on a bank statement now file straight into the Cheque tab (deposit/inward = Received, payment/outward = Issued), with the cheque number read automatically. Loans now reconcile themselves. When a bank statement contains a loan repayment (EMI / instalment / standing order), WealthFlow matches it to the right loan and files it against that month automatically — the same allocated-months method every other tab uses. If you had already marked that month paid, the statement's amount takes over as the authoritative figure. It's deliberately conservative: a row must clearly look like a loan (loan wording plus a matching bank, name or installment amount), so ordinary purchases are never mistaken for a loan payment. CC One-Time now sorts with two clean buttons — ↓ Newest and ↑ Oldest (default ↓ Newest) — so the controls never wrap and '+ Add Payment' stays put on small screens. The trained classifier also got much sharper: many more Sri Lankan merchants and services are recognised (supermarkets, restaurants, ride apps, e-commerce, pharmacies & hospitals, schools & courses, insurers, and streaming/SaaS subscriptions), so 'Goes to' and 'Type' are pre-selected correctly for many more rows. And statement balance lines written as B/F or C/F are now correctly skipped instead of leaking in as an expense. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.29.0: WealthFlow's credit-card brain just got sharper. Repayments and refunds on a statement (CASH PAYMENT-FINACLE, 'PAYMENT - THANK YOU', refunds, reversals) are auto-detected as a new Re-payment type and filed as a credit that pays down your charges oldest-first — and you can pick Re-payment manually too. Fee detection is far more accurate and AUTHORITATIVE: FUEL SURCHARGE, ADVANCE FEE, STAMP DUTY, annual & membership fees, finance/interest charges and every Sri Lankan tax/levy (VAT, NBT, SSCL, CESS) resolve to Service Fee, with a fee always winning over fuel & cash-advance — the trained engine is now the single source of truth in the import review, so a wrong guess can no longer mislabel a charge. Service Fee is a one-tap type everywhere. CC One-Time defaults to newest statement date on top, card filter chips are colour-coded per card, the 'LKR LKR' glitch is gone, recording a payment is locked to the card in context with thousands + .00 formatting, deleting a card's statements clears its orphaned payments, and Settings → Software Update → What's New always shows the notes now. Hard-refresh once after updating.
notes.7.28.0: Card-statement accuracy, levelled up. Every charge description is now classified by WealthFlow's OWN multi-AI engine: a built-in knowledge base instantly and correctly files the whole Sri Lankan fee family — DEBIT/CREDIT INTEREST, VAT, SSCL, NBT, CESS, GOVERNMENT LEVY, COMMISSION, LATE PAYMENT FEE, STAMP DUTY, FUEL SURCHARGE and FX mark-ups all become Service Fee (never Fuel), while real forecourts (Sinopec, Ceypetco, Lanka IOC, and any '… FILLING') stay Fuel — and anything it can't place is sent to a brand-new consensus endpoint where ALL configured engines (Gemini, Groq, DeepSeek, Mistral, Together, Fireworks, OpenRouter, Cerebras, SambaNova, NVIDIA, GitHub Models — plus the full 16-engine vote behind the scenes) decide by majority. The AI only ever upgrades a generic guess; locked fee/fuel/cash-advance verdicts are never overridden. Card repayment postings like CASH PAYMENT-FINACLE are now read as money INTO the card even when a statement hides the debit/credit column, so they settle charges instead of being mis-filed. The old bug that auto-marked imported card charges 'Paid' is now self-healing: on this update WealthFlow corrects any wrongly-Paid imported charge automatically (your manual settlements and real auto-matches are never touched), then re-settles only what your recorded payments truly cover, oldest charge first. The Type dropdown stays account-aware — bank accounts show only —, Service Fee and Fuel. Hard-refresh once after updating to load the fresh build.
notes.7.27.0: Credit-card auto-✅ matching is here. Record (or import) payments made to a card and WealthFlow settles your charges automatically — oldest charge first. A charge turns ✅ only when your payments fully cover it (and every older charge before it); an uncovered older charge holds back newer ones, exactly like a real card. Card payments (CASH PAYMENT FINACLE, 'PAYMENT - THANK YOU', re-payments) are now SAVED like any charge and shown in a new Card payments panel with a live paid/charged/surplus summary, plus a one-tap 'Record payment'. WealthFlow now also reads the two Sri Lankan card SMS alerts (a cash-advance/charge approval and a payment confirmation) and files them automatically. A new multi-model AI engine — where ALL configured engines vote in consensus (Gemini, Groq, DeepSeek, Ollama, Anthropic, Mistral, xAI, Together, Fireworks, OpenRouter, Cerebras, SambaNova, NVIDIA, GitHub Models, Cohere) — double-checks every statement line's Type and Category in the background for maximum accuracy, and now generalises to ANY bank statement and ANY card SMS (not just one format) — while stamp duty, fuel surcharge, cash-advance and ATM fees stay locked to Service Fee, so it only ever improves. The Type dropdown is now account-aware (bank accounts show —, Service Fee, Fuel). And the old bug that wrongly marked imported card charges 'Paid' after 50 days is fixed and auto-corrected on this update.
notes.7.26.0: Bank statements now read with bank-grade accuracy. For text-based PDF statements (NTB, DFCC, HNB) WealthFlow reads every line directly from the PDF and decides income vs expense from the running balance itself — the bank's own ground truth — so deposits, salary and inward CEFT transfers correctly land in Income and never as an Expense. Recognises all Sri Lankan statement date styles (04-Dec-2025, 01/05/2026, etc.). 'FUEL SURCHARGE' is now correctly a service fee (not fuel); only real fuel-station purchases are fuel. Charges imported from a credit-card statement are now logged as UNPAID amounts you still owe under the 50-day rule — they are no longer wrongly marked Paid.
notes.7.25.0: Bank-statement categorisation trained on real Sri Lankan statements (CEFT inward/outward transfers, POS merchants, cash deposits, expressway tolls, pharmacies, fuel) so far fewer rows land in Skip/Review. New per-card view in CC One-Time Payments. Real-time, accurate AI Engine Status. Statement imports no longer double-add cash-advance/fuel service fees.

7.24.1.headline: Bank-statement AI now reads income vs expense correctly — plus sharper Cloud Vision OCR

sections.title: Fixed (critical)
items: Imported statement rows are no longer all dumped into Expenses. The AI now reads each line's direction (money in vs money out) from the debit/credit column, the CR/DR marker, the amount sign, and the wording — so salary, deposits, interest, refunds and reversals land in Income (or as a card payment), never as an expense.
A refund or "PAYMENT - THANK YOU" on a card is now correctly treated as money in, even when the line also mentions a purchase or POS.

sections.title: Improved
items: Every row is identified before filing: known shops map to the right expense category, recurring services (Netflix, Spotify, Dialog, SLT, insurance, utilities) file under Subscriptions with the exact service named, and own-account transfers are skipped.
Subscriptions are now detected on credit-card charges too, not just bank debits.
When the AI genuinely can't tell what a row is, it no longer guesses silently — the row is flagged "Please review" in the import screen and sent to Settings -> Needs-Review so you decide.

Cloud Vision OCR accuracy raised: images are sharpened, upscaled and contrast-boosted before scanning, dense-document detection is used, and Sinhala/Tamil/English are all hinted — far fewer missed or misread rows on blurry or zoomed-out statements.
All AI engines keep running in parallel for consensus, so the extracted figures are cross-checked for accuracy.

7.24.0.headline: AI diagnostic fixed — now language-proof and honest about backend status

sections.title: Fixed
items: The Settings → "Run Diagnostic" check no longer shows a false "Degraded / result was unexpected" when your AI language is Sinhala or Tamil. It used to demand the literal English word "ONLINE"; because the AI answered in your language, the match failed even though the AI was working fine.

The diagnostic now probes the AI endpoint directly and reports the TRUTH: Online when the engine replies (in any language), the exact backend reason if it fails (including which provider API keys are missing), and Offline only when the endpoint is truly unreachable.

7.23.0.headline: Encrypted bank e-statements now truly open — plus far stronger OCR & polished AI text

sections.title: Fixed (critical)
items: Encrypted HTML e-statements (Nations Trust AMEX) now open and import correctly on EVERY device. Root cause: the decryption key used the wrong hash (SHA-256 instead of SHA-1), so the correct date-of-birth password was always rejected. Proven end-to-end against a real statement.

AI text now renders properly everywhere — **bold**, italics, bullets and headings show as formatting instead of raw * and ** symbols (CRIB analysis, score plan, debt strategy, simulations, cash-flow).
You can now delete a single expense, income or investment again — the floating AI button no longer covers the last row's delete/edit buttons.

sections.title: Improved
items: Statement & receipt photos that are blurry, dull or zoomed-out now read far more reliably: images are auto-enhanced (upscaled, contrast-stretched, sharpened) and run through Google Cloud Vision before AI extraction.
When the multi-engine scanner is unavailable, the app now gracefully falls back to Cloud Vision OCR + AI instead of showing an error — your scan still works.
CRIB report scanning now also auto-enhances images for sharper, more accurate reading.
The decryption library now loads with a strict timeout, so a slow network can never leave a statement stuck 'opening'.

sections.title: Polish
items: CRIB Credit Report screen now uses the same crisp line icons as the rest of the app (no more emoji), for a cleaner, more professional look.

sections.title: New
items: CRIB Credit Report: attach a report (PDF or photo) and get a deep AI analysis, in your chosen AI Response Language, in a warm human voice.
A separate action plan shows exactly how to raise your CRIB score, advance your risk category and strengthen your personal CRIB account.
Every report is tracked over time with a score ring, score-history line chart, outstanding/overdue trends, a latest-vs-previous delta table and a facilities breakdown.
Re-upload the same report and WealthFlow recognises it — it is not saved twice, but you still get the full analysis and comparison.
Re-analyse any past report in your current language, and export any analysis to a text file.

sections.title: Fixed
items: CRIB AI now reliably produces the analysis, advice, charts and graphs (the structured AI path no longer gets rewritten by the chat engine).
Duplicate CRIB reports are now correctly detected and rejected, using two independent fingerprints.
Comparison between past and present CRIB reports now works.
The Subscription tab no longer bleeds into Income, Investments, Expenses, Settings or any other tab — it now lives in its own page.
All AI replies now honour your selected AI Response Language app-wide (no more silent English fallback).

sections.title: Improved
items: Google Cloud Vision OCR now runs on every image AI call — receipts, one-time card charges, bank statements, CRIB and the AI advisor — for sharper, more accurate reading.
The AI advisor now knows and stays in sync with your CRIB reports, including deletions.
Your CRIB credit health now feeds into your WealthFlow score.

sections.title: New
items: Check for updates button and a full system self-check in Settings.
Update types are now labelled — Full, Minor, or Security.

sections.title: Improved
items: The update system now reliably detects newer versions and shows the banner.
Smoother, more accurate install progress.

sections.title: Security
items: June monthly security hardening for stored data and sync.

sections.title: New
items: In-app updates with What's New, agreement, PIN, backup-first and a live progress bar.
Send Feedback right from Settings.
Card & Account Registry is back.

sections.title: Security
items: All AI data encrypted at rest with AES-256-GCM.

sections.title: Fixed
items: System self-check no longer shows a false "CHECK" on Cloud sync — it now detects Firestore correctly (14/14).

sections.title: Security
items: June monthly security maintenance.

sections.title: New
items: Your feedback is now scored and prioritised automatically — security and crashes first.
Auto-install toggle for urgent security updates (like Android).
A red badge on Settings shows when an update is waiting.

sections.title: Improved
items: The update system is smarter and clearer, with full/minor/security labels.

sections.title: Security
items: Monthly security maintenance.

sections.title: New
items: Automatic feedback prioritisation and release scheduling.

sections.title: Security
items: Monthly security maintenance.

sections.title: New
items: Self-healing: if the app ever crash-loops after an update, it now auto-recovers to the last good version and keeps your data safe.
Smarter feedback understanding: reports are grouped by meaning, not just matching words.

sections.title: Security
items: Monthly security maintenance.

sections.title: Fixed
items: Privacy: the feedback view now shows only your own reports, never other users.
Feedback sent while offline now syncs and appears once you reconnect.
App version now shows consistently everywhere.
Software Update section reliably appears in Settings.

sections.title: New
items: Attach a screenshot to your feedback.

sections.title: Security
items: Per-user data isolation verified; monthly security maintenance.

sections.title: Fixed
items: The Software Update section now always appears in Settings — fixed a timing issue where it could be missing.

sections.title: Fixed
items: Swiping down from the top no longer reloads the app or shows an error — pull-to-refresh is now disabled.

7.15.3.headline: Software Update section no longer disappears

sections.title: Fixed
items: Clicking any button in the Software Update section no longer hides the whole section — it is now a permanent part of Settings.
App version is now consistent in the footer and sidebar too.

sections.title: Fixed (critical)
items: Fixed the black screen some users saw after updating. The app now has a Boot Guard that automatically clears the broken update and restores a working version — your data is always safe.

sections.title: Security
items: This is a mandatory recovery update.

7.16.1.headline: Critical: fixed frozen Syncing with cloud

sections.title: Fixed (critical)
items: Fixed the app freezing on Syncing with cloud and never letting you in. The cloud sync now has a strict time limit and always lets you into the app, even on a slow or offline connection.

sections.title: Security
items: Mandatory stability update.

notes.7.21.0: CRIB credit-report intelligence (AI analysis + advice in your language, dedup, history charts, score factor, delete-sync) · Google Cloud Vision OCR across the whole app (sharper reading of dense/zoomed-out statements) · encrypted HTML e-statements now open on every device (Nations Trust AMEX) · Income tab: matching card spacing + Table/Schedule views + sorting · AI now writes a useful note per imported transaction (no more "from statement") · repeated same-category charges accumulate per month with a tap-to-view history · faster startup · AI advisor now knows your CRIB + subscriptions + received income
